prompt: Cinematic single shot, 4 seconds. Start dark, end fully lit. The desk lamp gradually brightens from off back to full warm tungsten over the course of the clip. Time-coded motion: 0-1s: the scene rests in near-darkness, the lamp barely glows, the cards on the wall are silhouettes. 1-3s: the warm tungsten lamp smoothly and continuously brightens, the warm pool of light returns to the wall of pinned index cards, the cards re-emerge from shadow with their handwritten labels becoming legible, the warm tones return to the wood and ceramics. 3-4s: the lamp is at full warmth, a thin pale curl of steam has begun to rise actively from the ceramic mug, drifting slightly leftward in the now-warm air. Locked-off camera, no pan, no zoom, no cuts. No flicker, no electrical strobe. Real photographic motion, filmic. Steam ONLY rises upward, never downward. No text, no logos, no words. One smooth continuous brighten-up, like an operator returning, switching the lamp on, and the coffee starting to steam again.
